rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLYN S. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): No petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LISBETH INGRID PETER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/20. The issue of predeceased siblings are the nearest generation of issue living, which means that they take equally. [Probate Code Section 240.] The calculation of the fee base appears to be incomplete. See also CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6). A personal representative's duty is to account; if she hired someone else to prepare the accounting, that amount comes out of her statutory commission. Attorney costs paid out of the estate must be itemized. See disbursements made 3/20/19, 5/9/19, and 9/23/19. An amendment to the petition must be filed to address the issues above. Additionally, LRSF 2.11 M.4 provides that the original financial statements must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Please cite this language in the declaration. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.6 A.]. (The order appointing the administrator was dated 3/19/19. The proof of publication was filed on 11/6/18. The creditor claims period ended on July 21, 2019.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELDER SHARP GRAHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/20. The petitioner is an interested party; notice must be served by a disinterested party. The petition is incompletely verified (not dated). When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.26 D. and E.] (And who is "Tove" mentioned in the will?) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect creditors, even if the will waives, or all heirs waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000 A courtesy copy of a verified supplement to the petition, addressing the issues above and prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/4/20. A courtesy copy of the proof of service must also be provided.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GOLDFEIN FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 6, 1984
rulings.Calendar Matter: Report Of Sale And Petition For Order Confirming Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required pursuant to CRC 7.452. See also LRSF 14.3(A)(3) requiring counsel's appearance even if continuance is needed to correct procedural issues. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN BENJAMIN STECZYNSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/14/20. The date of the will is incorrect in item 3f(2) of the petition. Item 6f of the petition does not appear to be the first box that applies if siblings survived. Item 8 of the petition does not indicate if the predeceased sister was survived by issue. Francis T. Steczynski survived the decedent. Who are his successors-in-interest? (Did he die intestate?) Nothing has been filed to address the lost will. Probate Code Section 6124 provides that, "if the testator's will was last in the testator's possession, the testator was competent until death, and neither the will nor a duplicate original of the will can be found after the testator's death, it is presumed the testator destroyed the will with intent to revoke it. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence." It appears that an amended petition will need to be filed. An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. Courtesy copies of the amended petition, the proof of service of the amended petition, and proof of publication of the amended petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/4/20.
